name: pressure_fed_lox_kerosene_system_e63ffcf8
description: Design a pressure-fed rocket engine feed system using LOX (liquid oxygen) and kerosene as propellants, with nitrogen (GN2) as the pressurant gas. The system comprises three tanks (GN2, LOX, kerosene), pressurization lines from GN2 to each propellant tank, and feed lines from each propellant tank to the engine. The GN2 tank provides pressurization to maintain propellant tank pressures, driving propellant flow to the engine demand boundary. Component names are FIXED: gn2_tank, lox_tank, kerosene_tank, engine, gn2_to_lox_pressurization, gn2_to_kerosene_pressurization, lox_feed_line, kerosene_feed_line. Initial tank pressures, temperatures, masses, and component dimensions are TUNABLE for the designer to select.

notes: Use the pressure_fed_lox_kerosene seed as the starting point
Ensure GN2 pressure is sufficient to pressurize both propellant tanks
Ensure propellant tank pressures are sufficient to drive flow to engine
Both LOX and kerosene feed lines must deliver propellant to the engine
The downstream node named engine is an Ambient demand boundary for feed-flow checks.
Tune feed CdA values before changing topology.
If physical warnings mention a component, fix that component first.

checks.description: Simulation runs successfully without crashing or invalid configuration
No nonphysical or degenerate warnings during simulation
LOX feed line delivers propellant to engine (nonzero mass flow)
Kerosene feed line delivers propellant to engine (nonzero mass flow)
GN2 pressurization line to LOX tank has flow (nonzero mass flow)
GN2 pressurization line to kerosene tank has flow (nonzero mass flow)
LOX feed line delivers non-zero mass flow to the engine.
Kerosene feed line delivers non-zero mass flow to the engine.
